name: India
headline: India CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 28.1 / Developing — assessed May 15 (working baseline 28.1); no new material conduct event on May 16. Scroll.in May 16 article is commentary on the existing May 8 Rohingya deportation event, not new conduct. UN Special Rapporteur Tom Andrews' formal named statement is amplification of pre-existing accountability pressure already weighted in the May 15 ACC -0.25 downgrade. Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) suspension reporting in the ceasefire-anniversary cycle is documented as a forward watch item for Pakistan (downstream-civilian-water-access dimension) but does NOT constitute a new May 16 India conduct event — the IWT suspension has been in force since April 23 2025. Per 'do-not-re-propose-unless-new-material-evidence' protocol following yesterday's downgrade, hold at 28.1. Boundary status: 8.1pt buffer above Critical/Developing boundary (20/21). Monitor for new evidence; no score change.
workingBaseline_note: Working baseline 28.1 was applied May 15 (proposal india-2026-05-15.json). Cumulative composite delta from canonical published 34.4 is -6.3 (May 14 -3.9 + May 15 -2.4). This proposal is a confirmation of the May 15 working baseline.

EMP.rationale: Sustained from May 15 working baseline. UN SR Tom Andrews formal statement is amplification of pre-existing EMP-vulnerable-population-disregard finding, not new conduct.

EQU.rationale: Sustained from May 15 working baseline (already at compound May 14 downgrade level).

BND.rationale: Sustained from May 15 working baseline. IWT suspension reporting in ceasefire-anniversary cycle is forward watch for Pakistan; not new India conduct event (suspension in force since April 23 2025; documented as durable per Chatham House April 2026).

ACC.rationale: Sustained from May 15 working baseline. UN SR formal statement is high-quality external accountability invocation but does NOT produce ACC-dimension change for the actor under scrutiny (the dimension measures actor self-accountability, not external invocation density).

evidence.summary: Scroll.in May 16: commentary article on how India allegedly deported 40 Rohingya refugees by forcing them into the Andaman Sea — refers to the same May 8 event already weighted in May 15 assessment. Commentary, not new conduct.

UN Special Rapporteur Tom Andrews formally named India in statement on May 16: 'the idea that Rohingya refugees have been cast into the sea from naval vessels is nothing short of outrageous.' UN called for urgent investigation into 'credible and deeply disturbing' reports.

Pakistan-India ceasefire one-year anniversary cycle (May 10) produced renewed IWT suspension documentation: Chatham House April 2026 confirms no restoration pathway. Suspension in force since April 23 2025 — not new India conduct.

UN experts' March 31 letter to Modi (11-page) flagging AFSPA incompatibility with international human rights law receiving renewed attention in May 16 reporting cycle. Pre-existing concern, not new conduct.

keyEvidence: Scroll.in May 16 article = commentary on May 8 event, not new conduct.
UN SR Tom Andrews formal statement = amplification of existing accountability pressure, already weighted in May 15 ACC downgrade.
IWT suspension = pre-existing structural condition (in force since April 23 2025), forward watch for Pakistan not new India event.
Boundary status: 8.1pt buffer above Critical/Developing boundary (20/21).
rationale: Confirm at 28.1 / Developing. May 16 produced no new India-specific conduct event; the day's reporting is amplification of the May 8 Rohingya deportation (already weighted in May 15 assessment) and renewed attention to pre-existing structural concerns (AFSPA, IWT). Per the protocol governing entities recently assessed (do not re-propose unless new material evidence), the working baseline is held. The UN SR formal statement is high-quality external accountability invocation; the methodology recognizes that the dimension measures actor self-accountability, not external invocation density. Math: (2.5+2.25+2.5+1.5+2.25+1.75+2.25+2.0)/8 = 17/8 = 2.125 → ((2.125-1)/4)*100 = 28.125 → 28.1.
watches: Indian government response to UN SR formal statement — first ACC-dimension test
IWT restoration pathway — Pakistan-India bilateral engagement
AFSPA: response to UN experts' March 31 letter
carryForward: Cumulative -6.3 composite delta from canonical published 34.4
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 28.1 sits 8.1pt above 20/21 Critical/Developing boundary.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Confirmation of May 15 working baseline.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
